title: The Man Who Grew Too Much

jokes.text: We do have them! They're my home screen!
jokes.setup: Educational video about Neptune photos we hope to have from Voyager 2 in 1989
jokes.punchline: Student reveals they already have them on their phone
jokes.explanation: Clean anachronism joke that plays with time gap between old educational video and modern technology

jokes.text: It's Tuesday, Lisa. Taco Tuesday.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to give educational input to teacher
jokes.punchline: Teacher dismisses everything for food day
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing teacher's priorities and dismissive attitude

jokes.text: Taco Tuesday! Taco Tuesday! Taco Tuesday!
jokes.punchline: Students go into frenzied chant
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation comedy showing student mob mentality for food

jokes.text: Oh, no. I'm about to become another moppin' statistic.
jokes.setup: Lunch lady overwhelmed by Taco Tuesday chaos
jokes.punchline: Mops up spills becomes 'moppin' statistic'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels and fits the character's situation

jokes.text: There's only room for one of us, Ethel.
jokes.setup: Lunch lady overwhelmed by taco service
jokes.punchline: Dramatic standoff language applied to cafeteria work
jokes.explanation: Melodramatic tone applied to mundane situation creates absurd contrast

jokes.text: You know, Whitman says, 'If anything is sacred, the human body is sacred.' Fascinating.
jokes.setup: Lisa quotes poetry about sacred human body
jokes.punchline: Bart's deadpan dismissal while eating sixth taco
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - Lisa's intellectualism meets Bart's dismissive gluttony

jokes.text: Just don't ask how old the Jell-O is.
jokes.punchline: Ominous warning about Jell-O age
jokes.explanation: Escalates the gross-out food humor with implied ancient Jell-O

jokes.text: Whoa! Mom, you made me overswipe!
jokes.setup: Marge looking at tablet while Bart plays
jokes.punchline: Modern gaming terminology applied to accidental spending
jokes.explanation: Good satire of mobile game mechanics and parental obliviousness

jokes.text: Jasper Beardley! You'll never make it!
jokes.setup: Competitive rush for church volunteer signup
jokes.punchline: Marge treating church signup like action movie chase
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of competitive church lady behavior to action movie levels

jokes.text: What the hell is this thing?
jokes.punchline: Cursing in church about volunteer list
jokes.explanation: Character contrast of Marge swearing in church setting

jokes.text: You get to pass out abstinence pledges and make sure the kids sign their John Han-bleeps.
jokes.punchline: Ned censoring himself mid-pun
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned moment - makes dirty pun then censors himself, showing internal conflict

jokes.text: It was the only way I could avert... wine-tasting.
jokes.setup: Ned explaining why he's doing tango class
jokes.punchline: Wine-tasting treated as major crisis to avoid
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Ned's dramatic relationship with alcohol

jokes.text: Look, I'm really not comfortable talking about S-E-X with K-I-D-S.
jokes.punchline: Spelling out words like kids can't understand
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's discomfort and naivety

jokes.text: We won't have sex until we're married! To each other!
jokes.punchline: Clarification implies they might marry others
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reveals teenage thinking about marriage

jokes.text: Mm, their skipping lessons are really paying off.
jokes.punchline: Literal skipping lessons rather than ditching school
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection playing with double meaning of 'skipping'

jokes.text: Speaking of beautiful things, tongue me, babe.
jokes.setup: Marge calling abstinence a beautiful thing
jokes.punchline: Immediate crude sexual request
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe moment showing futility of abstinence message

jokes.text: How many of you are here for the free cookies? Because we've discontinued that.
jokes.punchline: Reveals and removes only incentive for attendance
jokes.explanation: Good setup-payoff showing how to lose an audience immediately

jokes.text: I'd like to play the first video that came up in my Web browser when I typed in 'GMO' plus 'danger' plus 'question mark.'
jokes.punchline: Admits to using terrible research methodology
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of internet research and confirmation bias, especially funny from smart character Lisa

jokes.text: If there's one thing scientists love, it's money. Money, money, money.
jokes.punchline: Reducing complex motivations to simple greed with repetition
jokes.explanation: Satirizes conspiracy theory rhetoric and oversimplification

jokes.text: in an unholy experiment in what is now a resort that charges $30 a night to valet park.
jokes.setup: Building up ancient Mayan experiments as ominous
jokes.punchline: Deflates to complaint about parking costs
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection from ancient doom to modern tourist complaints

jokes.text: The Mayans didn't listen... to themselves.
jokes.punchline: Logical impossibility of not listening to yourself
jokes.explanation: Absurdist logic that sounds profound but makes no sense

jokes.text: The answer is in your mouth!
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical dramatic conclusion
jokes.explanation: Parodies documentary false drama with meaningless revelation

jokes.text: My name is Jenny McCarthy, and I endorse this tirade.
jokes.punchline: Celebrity anti-vaccine advocate endorsing bad science
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to celebrity pseudoscience endorsements

jokes.text: Well, hurry, we have no minds of our own.
jokes.setup: Lisa telling students not to form opinions
jokes.punchline: Student openly admitting lack of independent thought
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about mob mentality and lack of critical thinking

jokes.text: I say, let's get these GMOs inside our children pronto!
jokes.punchline: Extreme enthusiasm for feeding children GMOs
jokes.explanation: Shows how quickly public opinion swings to extremes

jokes.text: I'm eating the food of the future. Beep, boop. What is love? Boop, beep, boop.
jokes.punchline: Acting like robot with existential questions
jokes.explanation: Absurdist take on GMO fears creating robotic behavior

jokes.text: Ooh, campus! Do you have a statue of the founder I can stick a funny hat on?
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate focus on statue vandalism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - reduces serious corporate tour to childish prank opportunity

jokes.text: Everyone, three molecules at a time.
jokes.setup: Homer saying one molecule at a time would take too long
jokes.punchline: Dramatically increasing to three molecules
jokes.explanation: Absurd corporate response to criticism - minimal change presented as major

jokes.text: Sideshow Bob!
jokes.punchline: Recognition of family nemesis
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup payoff with character recognition and shock

jokes.text: All right, I'll leave you and Bob in this locked room to get reacquainted.
jokes.punchline: Trapping family with known killer
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about corporate obliviousness to danger

jokes.text: That's not for you to suck!
jokes.punchline: Warning someone away from dangerous lab equipment
jokes.explanation: Visual gag implied from dialogue about lab safety

jokes.text: Either murder us or tell us how you got here.
jokes.punchline: Bart treating murder and exposition as equal options
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart line - cutting through dramatic tension with impatient pragmatism

jokes.text: The inanity of the vanity license plate puns was slowly driving me mad.
jokes.punchline: Sophisticated criminal undone by bad puns
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Bob's intellectual snobbery and vulnerability to wordplay

jokes.text: Look at me! I'm Sideshow Bob! Sideshow Bob!
jokes.punchline: Bart mockingly impersonating him
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart behavior - can't resist mocking even in danger

jokes.text: Foolish boy! That's phosphoric acid! A mere sip would have dissolved a hole in your stomach the size of a silver dollar.
jokes.punchline: Bob's dramatic warning about acid danger
jokes.explanation: Bob's theatrical warning style applied to lab safety

jokes.text: Lisa, you always were the one rose petal floating atop the cesspool that is the Simpsons.
jokes.punchline: Poetic insult to her entire family
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bob characterization - eloquent compliment that's simultaneously vicious insult

jokes.text: 'Overfed'? I don't know this word.
jokes.punchline: Homer genuinely not understanding the concept
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - his relationship with food makes 'overfed' incomprehensible

jokes.text: Don't you think the parts that aren't evil are a little, mm... pretentious?
jokes.punchline: Marge's diplomatic criticism of intellectualism
jokes.explanation: Good Marge moment - politely calling out intellectual pretension

jokes.text: Uh, that was a sexual harassment seminar. It doesn't matter-- I'm taking it pass-fail.
jokes.punchline: Reveals confusion about seminar type and academic attitude
jokes.explanation: Good Homer characterization showing his obliviousness and casual attitude

jokes.text: Well, we haven't had finger puppets in this church since Reverend Brogan was arrested.
jokes.punchline: Ominous implication about previous minister
jokes.explanation: Dark humor implying inappropriate puppet use by former minister

jokes.text: I prefer the modern thinking of Yolanda Yes.
jokes.punchline: Teen preferring promiscuous puppet
jokes.explanation: Predictable teen response subverting abstinence message

jokes.text: And I think I'd have a shot with Mabel Maybe.
jokes.punchline: Realistic assessment of romantic prospects
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing teen self-awareness about romantic prospects

jokes.text: Um, can I take one of those girl puppets home? I won't do nothin' to it.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate request with unconvincing denial
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor about teen desperation and poor impulse control

jokes.text: ♪ You say modified tomato, I say modified tomahto, I say engineered potato, you say engineered potahto ♪
jokes.punchline: Classic song adapted for GMO theme
jokes.explanation: Clever adaptation of classic song to episode theme

jokes.text: That is my first non-evil laugh in years.
jokes.punchline: Categorizing his own laughter by evil intent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob self-awareness about his villainous nature

jokes.text: It's like love. If it happens, it happens.
jokes.punchline: Comparing revenge to romantic love
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bob characterization - poetic approach to violence

jokes.text: That's why I got to get the marshmallows down fast.
jokes.setup: Homer ate all chocolate for s'mores
jokes.punchline: Logical response is eating marshmallows faster
jokes.explanation: Homer logic - solving eating mistake with more eating

jokes.text: I like how they cut them down from 140 characters to a more manageable 20 characters.
jokes.punchline: Twitter character limit reduced to impossible amount
jokes.explanation: Satire of social media constraints taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: It's why there's never been a teenage president.
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical presidential age requirement explanation
jokes.explanation: Homer logic connecting unrelated concepts with false causation

jokes.text: That means, three days ago, the two of you made the blob with two backs!
jokes.setup: Marge revealing two days of abstinence
jokes.punchline: Teen's dramatic Shakespearean reference to sex
jokes.explanation: Good literary reference combined with teen disgust

jokes.text: if you can't handle the image of our naked bodies in the tender act of a-oo-ga
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude description with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Homer's inappropriate oversharing with sound effects

jokes.text: Just get your fat heaving buttocks out of my head!
jokes.punchline: Teen's desperate plea to stop mental image
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe payoff to Homer's inappropriate sharing

jokes.text: The boy bands of the art world.
jokes.punchline: Bob's dismissive comparison to boy bands
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bob snobbishness - reducing classical art to pop culture criticism

jokes.text: That's using your $300,000 M.F.A.
jokes.punchline: Self-deprecating joke about expensive education
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware humor about education costs and practical application

jokes.text: Well, there is something I should have told you. You see, it occurred to me that if I can genetically modify fruits and vegetables, why can't I modify... me?!
jokes.punchline: Revelation of self-genetic modification
jokes.explanation: Good escalation revealing Bob's scientific megalomania

jokes.text: and a touch of corn for highlights on my hair.
jokes.punchline: Vanity-based genetic modification
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bob moment - using science for cosmetic vanity

jokes.text: Well, rhapsody in boo-hoo.
jokes.setup: Someone expressing disappointment about singing Gershwin
jokes.punchline: Bob's dismissive Gershwin pun
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bob wordplay combining classical music knowledge with dismissive attitude

jokes.text: Florence Nightingale's tenacity, though I'll have to clip around her moral fiber.
jokes.setup: Bob listing historical DNA to steal
jokes.punchline: Avoiding the moral parts of heroic figure
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Bob line showing his selective approach to improvement - wants skills but not ethics

jokes.text: Homo erectus? Where has that word been all my life?
jokes.punchline: Bart's juvenile delight in scientific name
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart moment - finding humor in scientific terminology

jokes.text: And we're scared of that because...?
jokes.setup: Bob threatening with zombie ant fungus
jokes.punchline: Bart's unimpressed response to threat
jokes.explanation: Great Bart deflation of villain theatrics with genuine confusion

jokes.text: They're all ugly because you're in them!
jokes.punchline: Bob's rage-driven insult triggering rampage
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Bob's violent transformation triggered by minor annoyance

jokes.text: and you don't see those people getting all violent.
jokes.setup: Wiggum discussing Middle East sexual taboos
jokes.punchline: Claiming Middle East isn't violent
jokes.explanation: Wiggum's obliviousness to global politics and violence

jokes.text: How dare you torture my children with Mozart!
jokes.punchline: Marge treating classical music as torture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - prioritizing cultural complaint over physical danger

jokes.text: I think I might be pregnant already.
jokes.setup: Marge offering to teach pregnancy prevention
jokes.punchline: Student revealing it's already too late
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about abstinence education failure

jokes.text: I don't want to die in some old dude's mouth!
jokes.punchline: Bart's specific complaint about death method
jokes.explanation: Good Bart response focusing on gross-out factor over death itself

jokes.text: And, Lisa, when you're older, write an autobiographical novel trashing the rest of them. How veiled? Thinly!
jokes.punchline: Encouraging her to write thinly-veiled family expose
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor about autobiographical fiction and family dynamics, perfectly in Bob's voice

jokes.text: Oh, right, I gave myself gills.
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of aquatic modification
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback subverting death scene with casual survival explanation

jokes.text: Sure do miss that laugh.
jokes.punchline: Missing specific detail rather than person
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment showing Ned's specific way of grieving

tmdb_overview: During a research trip, Lisa learns that Sideshow Bob is the chief scientist at a big chemical engineering company; Marge tries to teach a teen church group about healthy sexual practices.
